taskDescription: Drag the correct biochemistry terms into the blanks to complete these statements about lipid chemistry and metabolism.
textField:
Beta-oxidation of fatty acids occurs in the *mitochondria* and produces *acetyl-CoA*, NADH, and FADH2 in each cycle.

The rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol synthesis is *HMG-CoA reductase*, which is the target of *statins* — the most widely prescribed lipid-lowering drugs.

*LDL* is called bad cholesterol because it deposits cholesterol in arterial walls, while *HDL* is called good cholesterol because it performs *reverse cholesterol transport*.

Fatty acids are transported into the mitochondria by the *carnitine* shuttle system; the rate-limiting enzyme is *CPT-1*.

Lipids are classified into simple lipids (fats, waxes), *compound* lipids (phospholipids, glycolipids), and *derived* lipids (cholesterol, steroids).

*Ketone bodies* are produced in the liver during prolonged fasting or uncontrolled diabetes when acetyl-CoA accumulates from excessive beta-oxidation.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the lipid metabolism material and try again.
